objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: breakout_donchian_adx
archetype: breakout_donchian_adx
family: breakout
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- donchian(period=45)
- adx(period=10)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close > donchian.upper and adx.adx > 30
- short: close < donchian.lower and adx.adx > 30
exit:
- condition: cross_any(close, donchian.middle) or adx.adx < 25
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 2.25
tp_atr_mult: 3.0
description: ATR stop-loss (2.25x) and take-profit (3.0x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: Using a combination of Bollinger Bands, ATR and ADX for trend confirmation, as well as breaking out from Donchian Channels resistance levels, can provide a robust trading strategy with high signal quality.

pre_reflection.text: [Pre-reflection] The strategy is designed to use Donchian Channels, ATR, and ADX as indicators for trend confirmation. It enters long when the price breaks above the middle of the Bollinger Band, adx > 30 and exits either when it crosses below the lower band or the adx falls below 25. The strategy's take profit is set to triple ATR while stop loss is calculated as twice ADR multiplied by a multiplier for maximum drawdown protection. This strategy will be tested against historical market data. If results are poor, I would consider changing the entry criteria and/or adjusting parameters of the indicators.

iterations.hypothesis: La stratégie combine des signaux d'endommagement (Donchian), ATR pour le stop loss et take profit, ainsi qu'un seuil de forcement positif sur l'ADX.

pre_reflection.text: [Pre-reflection] I have reviewed the FICHE_STRATEGIE v1 strategy for breakout using Donchian Channels, ADX and ATR indicators in 1-hour timeframe. The parameters are set up to maximize risk reward ratio with stop loss based on 2.25x ATR and take profit at 3x ATR. The strategy is tested against the GBPUSD contract for last two iterations but not yet run its final backtest. For any poor results, I would consider changing or removing some parameters that may be causing suboptimal performance. If results are mediocre (low Sharpe), adjusting stop loss and take profit levels could help improve returns. As a fallback approach, using different indicators like RSI and EMA along with ATR can provide alternative strategy options.
